title: Departmental Contribution to Overhead
event: Reyes Co.'s West Department has sales of $300,000 and direct expenses of $210,000. Allocated indirect expenses are $50,000. Compute the department's contribution to overhead and explain which figure judges the manager.

computationPaths.narration: Judge managers on what they CONTROL: sales minus direct expenses. Allocated overhead is headquarters' arithmetic, not the manager's performance.

memorize.body: Contribution to overhead = departmental sales − DIRECT expenses. Allocated indirect costs come out only for full departmental income.
A department with positive contribution but negative income after allocations may still be worth KEEPING — eliminating it kills the contribution but not the allocated overhead.

questions.prompt: Sales $300,000, direct expenses $210,000, allocated indirect $50,000. Contribution to overhead = ?

distractors.feedback: That's income AFTER allocations. Contribution stops at controllable items: 300,000 − 210,000 = 90,000.
You subtracted only the allocated 50,000. Direct expenses (210,000) are the controllable costs that count here.
